---
title: Importing tokens
description: Import design tokens from Figma or other design systems into Subframe.
---

Import color tokens from existing design systems to match your brand. Subframe supports CSS variable import for colors only—typography, spacing, borders, and shadows must be configured manually.

## Importing color tokens

Import colors to individual palettes or non-palette tokens:

1. Navigate to **Components > Theme** in Subframe
2. Find the Colors section
3. Click **Import** (for non-palette colors) or click the import icon on a custom palette
4. Paste CSS variables in the dialog
5. Review and edit token names and values
6. Click **Import**

<Note>
Default palettes (like Brand and Neutral) don't support import. Create a new custom palette first, then import to it.
</Note>

## Supported CSS format

Paste CSS color variables, one per line:

```css
--color-token-name-100: #eeeeee;
--color-token-name-200: #dddddd;
--color-token-name-300: #cccccc;
```

Subframe parses CSS variables with this format:
- Must start with `--`
- Color value must be valid (hex, rgb, hsl)
- Semicolons optional

Variable names auto-convert to Title Case. `--color-brand-primary` becomes "Color Brand Primary".

## Import workflow

**Step 1: Paste variables** - Dialog shows textarea with example format. Paste your CSS variables.

**Step 2: Review and edit** - For each detected token:
- Edit palette name (applies to all tokens in import)
- Edit individual token names
- Adjust color values via color picker

**Step 3: Import** - Click **Import** to add tokens. They appear immediately in your theme.

<img src="https://developers.elementor.com/docs/assets/img/elementor-placeholder-image.png" alt="Import color tokens dialog showing the preview step with editable palette name, token names, and color values for multiple imported tokens" />

## Limitations

**Colors only** - Import works for color tokens. Typography, spacing, borders, and shadows require manual configuration.

**Valid colors required** - Non-color CSS variables are ignored during parsing.

**No Figma integration** - Export Figma variables to CSS using a plugin first, then paste into Subframe.

## After importing

**Sync to code** - Run `npx @subframe/cli sync --all` to update `tailwind.config.js` or `theme.css` with imported colors.

**Test across components** - Check that imported colors work with your components and have proper contrast.

## Troubleshooting

<AccordionGroup>
<Accordion title="No color tokens detected">

If import shows zero tokens:
- Verify CSS variables start with `--`
- Check color values are valid hex, rgb, or hsl
- Ensure proper format: `--name: value;` or `--name: value`
- Remove JavaScript syntax or preprocessor variables

</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Can't import to palette">

Import button only appears on custom palettes:
- Default palettes (Brand, Neutral) don't support import
- Click **New palette** to create a custom palette
- Then use the import icon on that palette

</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Palette name or token names empty">

Import requires all names to be filled:
- Edit palette name in the preview step
- Edit individual token names that are blank
- Click **Import** once all fields are valid

</Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
